findings.message: feedparser.parse() makes outbound HTTP calls to AWS RSS feed URLs; no timeout is configured so a slow feed can hang the workflow indefinitely
Reads GITHUB_OUTPUT env var and appends pipeline outputs to the file it points to — standard GitHub Actions pattern but represents env-var consumption
subprocess.run() passes RSS-derived title and body strings as arguments to gh CLI; shell=False prevents shell injection but untrusted content is not length-capped or sanitised
feedparser>=6.0.0 allows any future version including breaking major releases
requests>=2.28.0 is unpinned and not directly imported by either script (likely a transitive dep for feedparser)
PyYAML>=6.0 is unpinned and not imported by any script in this repository
'appropriately' in 'Configure timeout appropriately' — no measurable criteria given for what timeout value is appropriate
'appropriate' in 'Use appropriate models: Smaller models for simple tasks' — no measurable definition of 'appropriate' or 'simple'
'meaningful' in 'Set meaningful units for custom metrics' — 'meaningful' is undefined and unmeasurable
'proper' in 'Implement proper error handling' — 'proper' gives no actionable guidance
'appropriate' in 'Choose appropriate EBS volume type' — no criteria for which type to choose
'properly' in 'Configure health checks properly' — 'properly' gives no actionable criteria
'appropriate' in 'Set appropriate deregistration delay' — no guidance on what value to use
'meaningful' in 'Use meaningful source names — company.service.component' — 'meaningful' is vague; the example partially helps but the qualifier is still undefined
'appropriate' in 'Set appropriate timeout — don't use max 15 minutes unnecessarily' — no guidance on how to determine the value
'appropriate' in 'Configure appropriate backup retention' — no guidance on what retention period to choose
'appropriate' in 'Configure appropriate visibility timeout (> processing time)' — the hint '> processing time' helps but 'appropriate' remains vague
'appropriate' in 'Set appropriate maxReceiveCount (usually 3-5)' — 'appropriate' weakens the useful concrete range
'appropriate' in 'Set appropriate timeouts' — no criteria for what timeout values to use
'appropriately' in 'Configure timeout appropriately' — no measurable criteria given for what timeout value is appropriate
